text: Ruin. Septuagint katapontismou, "drowning," or to make the innocent suffer "shipwreck."

Thy, is not found in Hebrew. Doeg would not dare to enter the tabernacle, after he had slain the priests. (Calmet)<hr>Houbigant properly supplies thy. (Berthier)<hr>Living. The Jews inform us, that Doeg slew himself with his master at Gelboe, and that David punished his offspring with death. (Calmet)
Laugh, at the last day, when they will have nothing to fear, nor the wicked to hope. In this life, the just are full of compassion; but they cannot but approve of God's judgments. (Calmet)

Fruitful. David foretells his own prosperity on the throne, (Worthington) when this wretch shall be no more. (Haydock)<hr>He was at this time in great perplexity, (Calmet) in banishment from the house of God. (Menochius)
It punished the wicked, and asserted thy just providence. (Haydock)<hr>Good. Thy saints find the greatest comfort in thee. (Calmet)
